Green Party — Housing "A home for everybody" (current, from greens.org.nz/housing_campaign)

Everybody deserves an affordable, healthy place to call home, whether you rent or own. For the third of people in New Zealand who rent, that is not always the reality.

The Greens are focused on:
- Build Public Housing — public housing is the best way to make sure everybody has a safe, accessible, warm home; we've done it before in Aotearoa and can do it again (large-scale government house building).
- End Homelessness — pump support into the community and pull out all the stops to make sure everybody has a place to call home.
- Fix Renters' Rights — restore and strengthen renters' rights: ending no-cause evictions, stabilising rent increases, improving rental quality, and making renters' rights fairer.

Approach: government-led public/affordable house building, strong tenant protections, ending homelessness through housing-first support.
